summary_text: 1) **Recommended scope** **Fail-closed token-path hardening + local release handoff**. This gives the highest risk reduction now: it removes ambiguous legacy behavior (empty-token fallback) and makes receipt verification semantics deterministic before any further rollout. 2) **What to implement now** - Remove empty-token fallback in `task_backup` path resolution (`_mirror_path/_receipt_path` and all callsites). - Enforce `gha_dispatch_token` as mandatory for dispatch/reconcile; if missing, mark record failed with explicit `missing dispatch token` error (no silent fallback path). - Add/expand tests for: - missing-token records fail closed, - tokenized mirror/receipt paths are always used, - reconcile rejects any non-token canonical path. - Since `codex-kernel-guard` has no remote, produce a reproducible local handoff artifact now (`git format-patch` + checksum + apply instructions) so the change is transferable without ambiguity. 3) **What to defer and why** - **Receipt signing**: defer this turn. Proper signing needs key management/rotation/trust-anchor decisions (and secure key injection in workflow). Doing ad-hoc signing now adds complexity without reliable trust guarantees. - **Remote publication of `codex-kernel-guard`**: defer until remote/release path exists; local-only patch artifact is sufficient for this turn and avoids process risk.
